claims.claim: The Republic of Cyprus operates as a divided island nation with the internationally recognised government controlling the southern two-thirds whilst the self-proclaimed Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus administers the northern territory
claims.reasoning: Directly referenced in source [3] and reported by all three agents

claims.claim: The division stems from events in 1974, when Greek Cypriots attempted to annex the island to Greece, prompting Turkish military intervention
claims.reasoning: Directly referenced in source [3] and reported by all three agents

claims.claim: The Republic of Cyprus maintains that entry to the country must not occur through the northern territory
claims.reasoning: Directly referenced in source [3] and reported by all three agents

claims.claim: The Turkish lira serves as currency in the northern region
claims.reasoning: Directly referenced in source [3] and reported by all three agents

claims.claim: Cyprus is situated in the northeastern Mediterranean Sea at the crossroads of Europe, Asia, and Africa

claims.claim: The island lies approximately 40 miles south of Turkey, 60 miles west of Syria, and 480 miles southeast of mainland Greece

claims.claim: Nicosia serves as the administrative capital and primary financial centre
